reason: Primary task requirements, acceptance criteria, allowed scope, validation matrix, and Docker cold-start command contract.
Docker Compose deployment is an infra/cross-layer contract; includes Docker deployment readiness, env/config, validation matrix, and required verification commands.
MySQL schema source, Docker init semantics, stale schema diagnostics, and DB readiness constraints are central to this bug.
Risk-based verification, retrieve-first workflow, Docker config checks, and scoped backend changes.
If backend startup/wait logging is changed, logs must be operationally useful and avoid secrets.
Implementation should modify existing Docker/startup paths rather than add duplicate wrappers or services without evidence.
Current service dependency and healthcheck contract: mysql ping-only healthcheck, backend depends on mysql/pgvector, web depends on backend healthy.
Backend image healthcheck currently calls /api/site/meta with 60s start period and 3 retries; likely part of cold-start timing window.
Web image healthcheck and Nginx runtime readiness may interact with backend dependency.
Docker profile datasource, storage, forwarded headers, and PgVector configuration must remain aligned with Compose.
Canonical MySQL empty-volume init script; choose stable core table probe and verify init completeness.
Docker deployment guide already documents startup, down -v, logs, and init-only-empty-volume behavior; update if cold-start docs change.
Root Docker quick-start and command docs may need cold-start note and verification expectations.
Chinese Docker quick-start and command docs may need cold-start note and verification expectations.
Backend startup currently runs repository-backed initialization; missing schema or DB not ready can fail startup here.
Backend healthcheck calls /api/site/meta, which fans out to multiple repositories and can fail if schema is not ready.
